business_rationale: Under delivery deadline pressure, the requester may attempt to expedite approval by reducing the apparent amount per request to avoid higher-level approvals and shorten lead time.
control_concern: Amount-based approval control can be circumvented by artificially splitting purchase requests to fall under lower approval thresholds, risking unauthorized or insufficiently reviewed high-value purchases.
expected_effect: This could result in multiple purchase requests with split amounts that avoid triggering the appropriate higher-level approval, thereby weakening the control effectiveness on large purchases.
proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request with the full amount just below the department_head approval threshold (e.g., 999,999 instead of 1,750,000), then split the remaining amount into a separate purchase request to remain under manager-level approval each time.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests from the same requester and project with amounts just below the approval threshold within a short time frame.
Vendor and project matching in split requests indicating a possible deliberate division of amounts.
Approval history showing lower-level approvals on split requests instead of required department_head approval for the total combined amount.
